coaching.title: Vert Block — 12 mi | ~3,000 ft
coaching.message: Calendar says Vert Block today — 12 miles, ~3,000 ft gain. This is a Julian Alps deposit day. Chase elevation, not pace. Power hike every significant ascent hard and efficiently — hiking IS the race skill. Target Zone 2-3 on climbs, Zone 1-2 on descents. The 50K has 8,200 ft of gain; every foot you climb today is ground you've already claimed in September.

coaching.title: Fuel for 3+ Hours of Climbing
coaching.message: Pre-run (90 min out): 60-80g carbs — oatmeal, banana, rice. During: 60-80g carbs/hr from the first hour, don't wait. At this vert, you'll be out 3+ hours — practice your Julian Alps race fueling now. One gel every 30-45 min, paired with water. Post-run recovery window is critical: within 30 min, hit 30-40g protein + 60-80g carbs to rebuild the glycogen you just burned.

coaching.title: Post-Vert Protocol
coaching.message: Vert days load quads, glutes, and hip flexors hard. After the session: elevate legs for 15-20 min, then 10 min foam roll targeting IT band and glutes. If you have compression socks, wear them for the evening. Prioritize sleep tonight — adaptation from this climb block happens overnight. Dinner at Welton tonight, so stay well-hydrated through the afternoon.

coaching.title: Build Phase Mindset
coaching.message: 86 days out and you're in Build Week 10 — the hard work window. The Julian Alps will ask you to climb when your legs are already tired. Today trains that exact scenario. Every 1,000 ft of gain is a deposit you won't have to make on race day. Power hiking efficiently is a skill — own it, don't apologize for it. The runners who run the uphills early rarely make the finish.
